reason: BOUNDARY TERM ARGUMENT INCOMPLETE (lines 397-399): The proof states that boundary terms vanish 'since phi has compact support and the worldline eventually leaves any compact set.' This is an incomplete argument. A rigorous justification requires: (a) phi has compact support K; (b) gamma is proper (preimage of compact is compact, proved in Proposition 1 under the assumption gamma_j: R -> R^4); (c) therefore gamma^{-1}(K) is compact in R, hence bounded in [-T,T]; (d) for |tau|>T, phi(gamma(tau))=0; (e) hence boundary terms vanish. The proof OMITS the explicit invocation of properness (step b). It says the worldline 'eventually leaves any compact set,' which informally restates properness but does not cite Proposition 1 or its proof. Moreover, properness was proved only under the assumption gamma_j: R -> R^4 (Definition 2, line 65), while Definition 1 (line 45-51) allows gamma: I -> R^4 with I an arbitrary open interval. If I is bounded, properness can fail. The conservation proof implicitly requires gamma_j: R -> R^4 but does not state this prerequisite. This is a genuine gap since properness is load-bearing for the integration by parts.
